14	
15	# uv crate — `lib.rs`
16	
17	`crates/uv/src/lib.rs` is the library crate entry point of the `uv`
18	binary crate. It hosts the top-level `run` function that the `uv` /
19	`uvw` / `uvx` binaries call into, wires the `clap`-parsed `Cli` into
20	the appropriate command dispatch, and declares the in-crate sibling
21	modules that make up the CLI surface.
22	
23	## Module declarations
24	
25	The file declares the rest of the crate's modules:
26	
27	```rust
28	// from crates/uv/src/lib.rs
29	pub(crate) mod child;
30	pub(crate) mod commands;
31	#[cfg(not(feature = "self-update"))]
32	mod install_source;
33	pub(crate) mod logging;
34	pub(crate) mod printer;
35	pub(crate) mod settings;
36	```
37	
38	Each declared module is a sibling source file under
39	`crates/uv/src/`, so the set of `pub(crate) mod ...;` lines doubles
40	as a quick index of files in the crate.
41	
42	## Entry point
43	
44	```rust
45	// from crates/uv/src/lib.rs
46	#[instrument(skip_all)]
47	async fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
48	    // ...
49	}
50	```
51	
52	`run` receives a fully parsed [`Cli`] (from the `uv_cli` crate),
53	applies global flags such as `--directory` / `UV_WORKING_DIRECTORY`,
54	and then matches on `cli.commands` to call into one of the per-feature
55	command modules under [[uv-child]]'s sibling tree (`commands::pip`,
56	`commands::project`, ...).
57	
58	See also [[uv-child]] for the child-process signal handling that
59	`run` ultimately delegates to when a `uv run` / `uv tool run`
60	spawns a subprocess.
61

# uv crate — `lib.rs`

`crates/uv/src/lib.rs` is the library crate entry point of the `uv`
binary crate. It hosts the top-level `run` function that the `uv` /
`uvw` / `uvx` binaries call into, wires the `clap`-parsed `Cli` into
the appropriate command dispatch, and declares the in-crate sibling
modules that make up the CLI surface.

## Module declarations

The file declares the rest of the crate's modules:

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/lib.rs
pub(crate) mod child;
pub(crate) mod commands;
#[cfg(not(feature = "self-update"))]
mod install_source;
pub(crate) mod logging;
pub(crate) mod printer;
pub(crate) mod settings;
```

Each declared module is a sibling source file under
`crates/uv/src/`, so the set of `pub(crate) mod ...;` lines doubles
as a quick index of files in the crate.

## Entry point

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/lib.rs
#[instrument(skip_all)]
async fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<ExitStatus> {
    // ...
}
```

`run` receives a fully parsed [`Cli`] (from the `uv_cli` crate),
applies global flags such as `--directory` / `UV_WORKING_DIRECTORY`,
and then matches on `cli.commands` to call into one of the per-feature
command modules under [[uv-child]]'s sibling tree (`commands::pip`,
`commands::project`, ...).

See also [[uv-child]] for the child-process signal handling that
`run` ultimately delegates to when a `uv run` / `uv tool run`
spawns a subprocess.

2	title: venv Command
3	type: module
4	sources:
5	  - path: crates/uv/src/commands/venv.rs
6	  - path: crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
7	goals:
8	  - "list 2 commands in uv crate root"
9	created: '2026-05-10'
10	updated: '2026-05-10'
11	related:
12	  - "[[cache-clean]]"
13	  - "[[uv-commands-overview]]"
14	stale: false
15	---
16	
17	## from goal: list 2 commands in uv crate root (2026-05-10)
18	
19	`venv` is one of the top-level command entry points exposed by the `uv`
20	crate (see [[uv-commands-overview]]). It creates a Python virtual
21	environment, optionally seeding it with packages and respecting the
22	project's required-Python constraints.
23	
24	### Module shape
25	
26	The file defines a private `VenvError` enum that distinguishes the major
27	failure surfaces of the command:
28	
29	```rust
30	// from crates/uv/src/commands/venv.rs
31	#[derive(Error, Debug)]
32	enum VenvError {
33	    #[error("Failed to create virtual environment")]
34	    Creation(#[source] uv_virtualenv::Error),
35	
36	    #[error("Failed to install seed packages into virtual environment")]
37	    Seed(#[source] AnyErrorBuild),
38	
39	    #[error("Failed to extract interpreter tags for installing seed packages")]
40	    Tags(#[source] uv_platform_tags::TagsError),
41	
42	    #[error("Failed to resolve `--find-links` entry")]
43	    FlatIndex(#[source] uv_client::FlatIndexError),
44	}
45	```
46	
47	### Dependencies pulled in
48	
49	The module imports cross the boundary into many sibling crates, which
50	hints at how much the command orchestrates:
51	
52	- `uv_virtualenv` — actual venv creation, including the
53	  `OnExisting` policy for clobbering / preserving an existing folder.
54	- `uv_python` — interpreter discovery
55	  (`PythonInstallation`, `PythonRequest`, `PythonPreference`).
56	- `uv_dispatch::BuildDispatch` and `uv_resolver::FlatIndex` — used to
57	  install seed packages (e.g. `pip`, `setuptools`) into the new venv.
58	- `uv_workspace` — to discover the surrounding `VirtualProject` so the
59	  venv honors the workspace's `requires-python`.
60	- `uv_shell::{shlex_posix, shlex_windows}` — for printing
61	  shell-appropriate activation hints after creation.
62	
63	### Re-export
64	
65	The function is re-exported from `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`:
66	
67	```rust
68	// from crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
69	pub(crate) use venv::venv;
70	```
71

---

## from goal: list 2 commands in uv crate root (2026-05-10)

`venv` is one of the top-level command entry points exposed by the `uv`
crate (see [[uv-commands-overview]]). It creates a Python virtual
environment, optionally seeding it with packages and respecting the
project's required-Python constraints.

### Module shape

The file defines a private `VenvError` enum that distinguishes the major
failure surfaces of the command:

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/commands/venv.rs
#[derive(Error, Debug)]
enum VenvError {
    #[error("Failed to create virtual environment")]
    Creation(#[source] uv_virtualenv::Error),

    #[error("Failed to install seed packages into virtual environment")]
    Seed(#[source] AnyErrorBuild),

    #[error("Failed to extract interpreter tags for installing seed packages")]
    Tags(#[source] uv_platform_tags::TagsError),

    #[error("Failed to resolve `--find-links` entry")]
    FlatIndex(#[source] uv_client::FlatIndexError),
}
```

### Dependencies pulled in

The module imports cross the boundary into many sibling crates, which
hints at how much the command orchestrates:

- `uv_virtualenv` — actual venv creation, including the
  `OnExisting` policy for clobbering / preserving an existing folder.
- `uv_python` — interpreter discovery
  (`PythonInstallation`, `PythonRequest`, `PythonPreference`).
- `uv_dispatch::BuildDispatch` and `uv_resolver::FlatIndex` — used to
  install seed packages (e.g. `pip`, `setuptools`) into the new venv.
- `uv_workspace` — to discover the surrounding `VirtualProject` so the
  venv honors the workspace's `requires-python`.
- `uv_shell::{shlex_posix, shlex_windows}` — for printing
  shell-appropriate activation hints after creation.

### Re-export

The function is re-exported from `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`:

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
pub(crate) use venv::venv;
```

content.text:
- **uv-lib.md** — 說明 `crates/uv/src/lib.rs` 作為 `uv` binary crate 的 library 入口，宣告 sibling 模組並提供 `run` 函式來分派 `clap` 解析後的 CLI 命令。
- **venv.md** — 介紹 `uv venv` 命令的實作位置與 `VenvError` 失敗分類，並列出它跨入 `uv_virtualenv`、`uv_python`、`uv_dispatch` 等 sibling crate 來建立並 seed 虛擬環境。

result:

- **venv.md** — 介紹 `uv venv` 命令的實作位置與 `VenvError` 失敗分類，並列出它跨入 `uv_virtualenv`、`uv_python`、`uv_dispatch` 等 sibling crate 來建立並 seed 虛擬環境。
